questions.question: The currency period of the money order shall expire upon the:

options.A: End of the last day of the second month following the month of issue
options.B: End of the last day of month of issue
options.C: End of 3 months from the date of issue
options.D: End of 6 months from the date of issue

questions.explanation: According to Post Office guidelines, the currency period of a money order expires at the end of the last day of the second month following the month of issue.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: ___ is the name given by India Post to the facility of franking based on RMFS.
questions.explanation: Digifrank Plus is the branding given by India Post for the franking facility based on Remotely Managed Franking System (RMFS).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the validation period for Franking Machine License?
questions.explanation: A Franking Machine License issued by the Department of Posts is valid for a period of 5 years.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be posted in the letter box?
questions.explanation: Registered newspapers cannot be posted in ordinary letter boxes; they must be handed over at the post office counter.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The public are advised to use the minimum number of stamps while prepaying postage charges by using stamps of the ___ appropriate denominations.
questions.explanation: To save space on the postal article and simplify cancellation, the public is advised to use the minimum number of stamps by choosing higher appropriate denominations.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What are the modes of payment of the postage or other sums chargeable for mail services?
questions.explanation: Postage and other charges can be paid via cash, digital modes, or by affixing valid postage stamps.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Post Boxes are available on rent at certain Post Offices as may be determined by the:
questions.explanation: The availability of Post Boxes on rent at specific Post Offices is determined by the Head of the Postal Circle.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is rate of the postage chargeable on the delivery of unpaid official postal articles?
questions.explanation: For official postal articles sent unpaid, the postage chargeable on delivery is the standard pre-paid rate, unlike private unpaid articles which are charged double.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following are prohibited for transmission by the Inland post?

options.A: Which is sent in contravention of any enactment for the time being in force
options.C: Ticket, proposal or advertisement relating to a lottery

questions.explanation: The Post Office Act prohibits the transmission of illegal, obscene, or lottery-related materials through inland post.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Imported postal article containing coin, bullion, precious stones, jewellery, article of gold or silver or other articles which are liable to customs duty:

options.A: Will not be transmitted until customs duty is paid
options.B: Will be transmitted even if customs duty is not paid

questions.explanation: Any imported postal article liable to customs duty will be withheld from delivery or onward transmission until the required customs duty is paid.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the compensation for delay in speed post delivery?
questions.explanation: In case of a delay in the delivery of a Speed Post article beyond the prescribed norms, the compensation provided is the refund of the Speed Post charges paid.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the rate of penalty, if a bulk customer fails to make the payment of BNPL bill by due date?
questions.explanation: For Book Now Pay Later (BNPL) bulk customers, a penal interest of 12% per annum is levied on the default amount if the bill is not paid by the due date.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: If the COD value to be recovered from addressee is up to Rs 6500, then what is the additional fee which the sender shall be required to pay for transmitting postal article as Cash-on-Delivery (COD) article?

options.A: 1.6% of COD value to be recovered
options.B: 1.5% of COD value to be recovered
options.C: 1% of COD value to be recovered
options.D: 2% of COD value to be recovered

questions.explanation: For Cash-on-Delivery (COD) articles where the recovery value is up to Rs 6500, the fee is generally calculated at 1.6% of the COD value to be recovered (minimum Rs 50).
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following is not true about Cash-on-Delivery (COD) facility?

options.A: It provides collection up to Rs 50000
options.C: Such articles do not contain coupons, tickets, certificates or introductions designed for the sale of goods on what is known as the Snowball System

questions.explanation: Insurance is not strictly compulsory for all COD articles; it depends on the nature and value of the contents. Other statements given are correct rules for COD.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Maximum number of individuals that may be nominated as nominee in Post Office Savings Account is:
questions.explanation: A maximum of 4 individuals can be nominated as nominees in a Post Office Savings Account.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following statements is not correct about Post Office Savings Account?

options.A: Only one account can be opened by an individual as a single account
options.B: Account can be opened by minor who has attained the age of 10 years
options.C: There is no limit of maximum deposit
options.D: An account opened as a single account can subsequently be converted into joint account

questions.explanation: Under the current Post Office Savings Bank (POSB) rules, a single account cannot be converted into a joint account, and vice-versa.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the fee for cancellation or change in nomination in any account under Post Office Savings Scheme?
questions.explanation: As per the specific official answer key provided for this PYQ set, the answer is 'No fee' (D), though historically nominal fees have been subject to rule changes.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: What is the correct statement about loan facility in Post Office Recurring Deposit Account?

options.A: Loan amount is limited to 50% of deposit
options.B: Account should have been operational for at least one year
options.C: Twelve monthly deposits should have been made in the account

questions.explanation: In a Post Office RD account, a loan up to 50% of the balance is allowed only after the account has been active for one year and 12 monthly installments have been deposited.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: The investment under ___ years TD qualifies for the benefit of section 80C of Income Tax Act, 1961.
questions.explanation: Only the 5-Year Time Deposit (TD) in the Post Office qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Retired personnel of Defence Services (excluding Civilian Defence employees) shall be eligible to open an account under Senior Citizens Savings Scheme on attaining the age of ___ years subject to the fulfilment of other specified conditions.
questions.explanation: Defence personnel (excluding civilian defence employees) can open an SCSS account upon reaching 50 years of age, provided they meet other conditions.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: If SCSS account closed on or after 2 years, an amount equal to ___ of the deposit shall be deducted and the balance shall be paid to the account holder.
questions.explanation: For a Senior Citizens Savings Scheme (SCSS) account, if it is closed after 2 years from the date of opening, a penalty of 1% of the deposit amount is deducted.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Loan facility is available in Joint Life Assurance (Yugal Suraksha) under PLI after:
questions.explanation: Under Postal Life Insurance (PLI), the loan facility for a Joint Life Assurance (Yugal Suraksha) policy becomes available after the completion of 3 years.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following PLI policy does not have surrender facility?
questions.explanation: The Children Policy under PLI (Bal Jeevan Bima) does not have a surrender facility.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which orders are issued by Superintendent RMS Division for prescribing changes in sorting lists?
questions.explanation: 'A' orders are issued by the Superintendent of RMS to prescribe changes in sorting lists and mail routing.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: An ___ is used between a Sub-Post Office and its Head Post Office to enclose the bags and articles, documents, etc., connected with accounts as well as correspondence unconnected with accounts, from the Head Post Office to one of its Sub-Post Offices and vice versa.
questions.explanation: An Account Bag is specifically used for exchanging cash, accountable articles, and documents between a Sub-Post Office and its parent Head Post Office.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which statement is true about Transit Bag?
questions.explanation: A Transit Bag is a 'due bag' (must be dispatched regularly) and it must contain a mail list detailing the enclosed bags.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: ___ is used to enclose several bags sent to the same office or section, thereby affording protection to them, and securing the disposal in transit of only one bag in place of several.
questions.explanation: A Transit Bag is used to enclose multiple bags destined for the same office, reducing the number of individual bags handled in transit.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following offices does not deal with any bags but only deals with the correspondence relating to procurement and distribution of bags?
questions.explanation: The Central Bag Office (D Directorate level) does not physically handle bags but manages correspondence, procurement, and overall distribution policy.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Stationary office situated at the Headquarters of RMS Division which, in addition to the ordinary duties of a Record Office, is entrusted with the preparation of salary and contingent bills for the entire Division and the accounts connected therewith is termed as:
questions.explanation: A Head Record Office (HRO) performs standard record office duties plus administrative and accounting tasks like salary and contingent bills for the RMS division.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Office which does not exchange mails with the offices in foreign countries but in which the work of customs examination, assessment and accounting of customs duty is carried out is called:
questions.explanation: A Sub Foreign Post Office facilitates customs examination and assessment for local delivery but does not independently exchange direct mail bags with foreign countries.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Which of the following imaginary line almost divides India into two equal parts?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer (23.5° N) passes through the middle of India, dividing it roughly into two equal halves (tropical and subtropical zones).
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Which one of the following is the place of confluence of the Alaknanda and the Bhagirathi Rivers?
questions.explanation: Devprayag is the sacred confluence where the Alaknanda and Bhagirathi rivers meet to form the holy river Ganga.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: Which house of Parliament is elected directly by the people?
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha (House of the People) is the lower house of India's bicameral Parliament, with members elected directly by the citizens.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: What does Article 19 of the Indian Constitution guarantee?

options.B: Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression

questions.explanation: Article 19 guarantees several freedoms to Indian citizens, primarily the Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression.
questions.subject: Polity

questions.question: Volcanic eruption does not occur in the:
questions.explanation: The Baltic Sea is located on a stable continental shield (Baltic Shield) far from tectonic plate boundaries, meaning no volcanic activity occurs there.
questions.subject: Geography

questions.question: The hottest planet in the solar system?
questions.explanation: Despite Mercury being closer to the Sun, Venus is the hottest planet due to a dense atmosphere of carbon dioxide causing a massive greenhouse effect.
questions.subject: Science

questions.question: During which movement did Mahatma Gandhi first use the term 'Satyagraha' to denote his philosophy of non-violent resistance?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi first used 'Satyagraha' in India during the Champaran Satyagraha of 1917 to protest against the indigo planter system.
questions.subject: History

questions.question: Which Indian Academy is promoting dance, drama and music?
questions.explanation: The Sangeet Natak Akademi (Sangeet Academy) is the national level academy for performing arts set up by the Government of India to promote dance, drama, and music.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Ethics determines rightness and wrongness of:
questions.explanation: Ethics is the branch of philosophy that deals with the moral principles governing human actions and behavior.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Children learn moral values through:
questions.explanation: Children internalize moral values effectively through a combination of active cognitive thinking and logical reasoning about situations.
questions.subject: General Awareness

questions.question: Solve the following: 20 ÷ 30 ÷ 15 × 10 ÷ 10 × (-3)
questions.explanation: Based on the official answer key provided for this specific paper, the answer is A. Note: The mathematical expression in the source may contain a typographical error from the original exam standard.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an item at Rs. 840 at a gain of 20% and another item for Rs. 960 at the loss of 4%. Find his total gain percent.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: A man bought an article and sold it at a gain of 5%. If he had bought it at 5% less and sold it for Re 1 less, he would have made a profit of 10%. The C.P. of the article was:
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Rahul took a loan of Rs. 1200 with simple interest for as many years as the rate of interest. If he paid Rs. 432 as interest at the end of the loan period, what was the rate of interest?
questions.explanation: SI = P × R × T / 100. Here T = R. So, 432 = 1200 × R² / 100. This simplifies to 432 = 12 × R², meaning R² = 36. Therefore, R = 6%.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: In the first 10 overs of a cricket game, the average run rate was only 3.5 per over, then what should be the run rate in the remaining 40 overs to reach the target of 275 runs?
questions.explanation: Runs scored in 10 overs = 10 × 3.5 = 35. Runs needed = 275 - 35 = 240. Required run rate for 40 overs = 240 / 40 = 6.0 runs per over.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: A can do a work in 15 days and B in 20 days. If they work on it together for 5 days, then the fraction of the work that is left is:
questions.explanation: Work done by A in 1 day = 1/15. By B = 1/20. Together = 1/15 + 1/20 = 7/60. Work done in 5 days = 5 × (7/60) = 35/60. Work left = 1 - 35/60 = 25/60 = 5/12.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: 'A' alone can finish a piece of work in 70 days. 'B' is 75% more efficient than A. How many days will B alone take to finish the work?
questions.explanation: Ratio of efficiency of A to B is 100 : 175 = 4 : 7. Ratio of time taken is 7 : 4. If 7 units = 70 days, then 4 units = 40 days.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: Excluding stoppages, the speed of a bus is 54 kmph and including stoppages, it is 45 kmph. For how many minutes does the bus stop per hour?
questions.explanation: Stoppage time per hour = (Difference in speed) / (Speed without stoppage) = (54 - 45) / 54 = 9/54 = 1/6 of an hour. 1/6 × 60 minutes = 10 minutes.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability

questions.question: If the cost of X meters of wire is 'M' rupees, then find the cost of 'Y' meters of wire at the same rate.
questions.explanation: Cost of 1 meter of wire = M / X. Cost of Y meters of wire = Y × (M / X) = MY / X.
questions.subject: Numerical Ability
